title: ferray-linalg: no complex matrix support — LinalgFloat is sealed to f32/f64
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's linalg fully supports complex matrices: np.linalg.solve(complex_A, complex_b), np.linalg.eig(complex_A), np.linalg.svd(complex_A), np.linalg.det(complex_A), etc. The LAPACK routines used are zgesv, zgeev, zgesdd (z = complex double). ferray-linalg's LinalgFloat trait is sealed to f32 and f64 — Complex<f32> and Complex<f64> are excluded. This means no complex linear algebra at all. faer supports complex types natively (faer::complex::c64), so this is a trait-bound restriction, not a backend limitation. Extend LinalgFloat (or add a LinalgScalar supertrait) to accept Complex<f32>/Complex<f64>.
